Class QueryMapper.Field

java.lang.Object
org.springframework.data.cassandra.core.convert.QueryMapper.Field
Direct Known Subclasses:
QueryMapper.MetadataBackedField
Enclosing class:
QueryMapper
protected static class QueryMapper.Field extends Object
Value object to represent a field and its meta-information.
Since:
2.0
Author:
Mark Paluch

  • Field Summary

    Field
    Description
    protected final ColumnName
    name
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ColumnName
    getMappedKey()
    Returns the key to be used in the mapped document eventually.
    CassandraPersistentProperty

    Returns the underlying CassandraPersistentProperty backing the field.
    boolean
    hasProperty()
    Return true if the field is backed by a CassandraPersistentProperty.
    boolean
    hasProperty(Predicate<CassandraPersistentProperty> predicate)
    Return true if the field is backed by a CassandraPersistentProperty and matches the given CriteriaDefinition.Predicate.
    QueryMapper.Field
    with(ColumnName name)
    Returns a new QueryMapper.Field with the given name.

  • Field Details

    • name

      protected final ColumnName name

  • Method Details

    • with

      public QueryMapper.Field with(ColumnName name)
      Returns a new QueryMapper.Field with the given name.
      Parameters:
      name - must not be null or empty.
      Returns:
      a new QueryMapper.Field with the given name.

    • hasProperty

      public boolean hasProperty()
      Return true if the field is backed by a CassandraPersistentProperty.
      Returns:
      true if the field is backed by a property; false otherwise.
      Since:
      5.1

    • hasProperty

      public boolean hasProperty(Predicate<CassandraPersistentProperty> predicate)
      Return true if the field is backed by a CassandraPersistentProperty and matches the given CriteriaDefinition.Predicate.
      Returns:
      true if the field is backed by a property and matches the given predicate; false otherwise.
      Since:
      5.1

    • getProperty

      public CassandraPersistentProperty getProperty()
      Returns the underlying CassandraPersistentProperty backing the field. For path traversals this will be the property that represents the value to handle. This means it'll be the leaf property for plain paths or the association property in case we refer to an association somewhere in the path.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the field is not backed by a property.

    • getMappedKey

      public ColumnName getMappedKey()
      Returns the key to be used in the mapped document eventually.
